seoTitle: Gray vs Gregory — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gray and Gregory. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gray and Gregory.
heading: Gray vs Gregory — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gray, Northern Territory and Gregory, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gregory has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Gray (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gray or Gregory safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gray has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Gregory scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gray scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Gregory scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gray or Gregory?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gregory has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
